dsl_rule.translation_notes: Flag formation detection uses Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to identify the consolidation boundaries and breakout point. Volume confirmation ensures legitimate breakout. Entry triggers on price crossing Donchian bands. Exit uses pivot support/resistance levels as proxy for stop-loss and target price calculation based on formation height. Context-dependent direction reflects that flags are continuation patterns—direction determined by prior trend. Success rate of 64% for bull flags translates to 2% stop-loss and 20-day hold window to capture 23% average rise.

source_rule_text: Flag Formation: A continuation pattern where price consolidates after a strong move, followed by a breakout in the original direction of the trend
